keyPoints: Sockets are endpoints for communication between two devices (computer, phone, etc.) in a network.
TCP/IP sockets use connection-oriented protocols, establishing a connection before data is sent.
TCP sockets use acknowledgment packets to ensure data is received correctly.
UDP sockets use no acknowledgment packets, relying on checksums to ensure data integrity.
Bind function assigns a socket to a specific address and port.
Listen function allows a socket to accept incoming connections.
Accept function receives an incoming connection and returns a socket object.
